title: Polygon ID / Privado ID Vocabulary
description: Core terms and concepts used across the Polygon ID (Privado ID) issuer node and verifier backend APIs.

terms.label: Decentralized Identifier
terms.definition: A globally unique persistent identifier that does not require a centralized registration authority. Used by the Polygon ID protocol as the primary identity handle for issuers, holders, and verifiers.

terms.label: Verifiable Credential
terms.definition: A tamper-evident credential that has been cryptographically signed by an issuer. Used to assert claims about a subject (the holder) without exposing raw personal data.
terms.example: A KYC credential asserting that a wallet address belongs to a verified adult.

terms.label: Zero-Knowledge Proof
terms.definition: A cryptographic primitive allowing a prover to demonstrate knowledge of a value or satisfaction of a predicate without revealing the underlying data. Core to privacy-preserving verification in Polygon ID.
terms.example: Proving age > 18 without disclosing the actual birth date.

terms.label: Merkle-Tree Proof
terms.definition: A proof of membership in a Sparse Merkle Tree used by the iden3 protocol to prove that a credential is included in (or excluded from) an identity's credential Merkle tree.
terms.example: Inclusion proof that credential ID 0x1234 exists in the issuer's claims tree.

terms.label: Baby Jubjub Key
terms.definition: An elliptic curve optimised for ZK-SNARK circuits. Polygon ID uses BJJ keys as the primary signing key type for issuers and identity wallets.
terms.example: babyjubjub key pair generated during identity creation.

terms.label: Ethereum Key
terms.definition: Standard Ethereum-compatible (secp256k1) key type supported alongside BJJ keys in the Issuer Node key management system.
terms.example: Ethereum key used for on-chain state publication transactions.

terms.label: JSON Web Zero-Knowledge
terms.definition: A token format derived from JSON Web Token (JWT) that carries a ZK proof instead of a standard signature. Used in the Polygon ID auth flow to authenticate users to verifiers.
terms.example: JWZ token sent by a wallet in response to an authorization request.

terms.label: Identity State
terms.definition: The on-chain commitment to an identity's current credential and revocation Merkle trees. Issuers must publish state transitions to the blockchain after issuing or revoking credentials.
terms.example: State hash published to the Polygon network after issuing the first credential.

terms.label: Credential Schema
terms.definition: A JSON-LD document defining the structure of a verifiable credential type. Stored in the Issuer Node schema registry and referenced by credentials at issuance time.
terms.example: KYCAgeCredential schema defining the birthday field.

terms.label: Display Method
terms.definition: An optional reference to a rendering template (e.g. an SVG or URL) that wallets use to visually display a credential to the holder.
terms.example: Iden3BasicDisplayMethodV1 pointing to an SVG card template.

terms.label: Revocation Status
terms.definition: The endpoint or Merkle-tree path through which a verifier can check whether a credential has been revoked by its issuer.
terms.example: Iden3ReverseSparseMerkleTreeProof endpoint on the issuer node.

terms.label: Authorization Request
terms.definition: A QR-code payload or Universal Link generated by a verifier that asks a wallet holder to prove one or more claims. Encodes the ZK query constraints.
terms.example: QR code displayed on a DeFi application asking for proof of KYC.

terms.label: Issuer-Holder Connection
terms.definition: A persistent record in the Issuer Node representing the link between an issuer DID and a holder DID established during a wallet authentication flow.
terms.example: Connection record created after a holder scans the issuer's auth QR code.

terms.label: Credential Offer Link
terms.definition: A shareable short URL generated by the Issuer Node that carries a credential offer. Holders can scan or click the link to trigger credential issuance to their wallet.
terms.example: Link with expiry used in an email campaign to distribute KYC credentials.

terms.label: Payment Request
terms.definition: An iden3comm message extension allowing an issuer to request on-chain payment (ERC-20, ERC-721, or native) as a precondition for credential issuance.
terms.example: ERC-20 payment request for $5 USDC before issuing a credential.

terms.label: iden3 Protocol
terms.definition: The open cryptographic identity protocol developed by the Hermez / Polygon team. Defines the Sparse Merkle Tree structure, ZK-proof circuits, and messaging protocol used by Polygon ID.
terms.example: iden3comm messaging used between wallet and issuer node.
